title: The Fed Has Been Honest and Stupid
content: It would be easy—and a mistake—to blame the US Federal Reserve’s many blunders on corrupt bureaucrats or imagined conspiracies. The truth of the matter is that the central bank relies on an outdated regional map and lagging indicators that no longer tell the whole story, while battling macroeconomic blazes that politicians stoke.
snippet: It would be easy—and a mistake—to blame the US Federal Reserve’s many blunders on corrupt bureaucrats or imagined conspiracies. The truth of the matter is that the central bank relies on an outdated
creator: Todd G. Buchholz
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A New Framework for Climate Displacement
content: Global warming is already uprooting communities and impoverishing people who remain in vulnerable areas—a crisis that is often overshadowed by the focus on decarbonization. But the Global Climate Mobility Principles offer policymakers a framework for ensuring that these individuals maintain their agency and rights.
snippet: Global warming is already uprooting communities and impoverishing people who remain in vulnerable areas—a crisis that is often overshadowed by the focus on decarbonization. But the Global Climate
creator: Carlos  Alvarado-Quesada
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Peace With Iran Is All About Lebanon Now
content: US President Donald Trump says that he "calls the shots" on negotiating with Iran, and Israel has no choice but to submit to a deal that undermines its security interests. But a deal that makes Trump the protector of Lebanon—and, by extension, Iran-backed Hezbollah—could put the US and Israel on a collision course.
snippet: US President Donald Trump says that he "calls the shots" on negotiating with Iran, and Israel has no choice but to submit to a deal that undermines its security interests. But a deal that makes Trump
creator: Shlomo Ben-Ami
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Every British Prime Minister’s Nightmare
content: The true nature of Britain’s “special relationship” with the United States has far more to do with American financiers’ willingness to keep borrowing to purchase British government debt than with history, culture, or Britain’s defense needs. Every UK Prime Minister must bear this burden, which is rapidly becoming unsustainable.
snippet: The true nature of Britain’s “special relationship” with the United States has far more to do with American financiers’ willingness to keep borrowing to purchase British government debt than with
creator: Yanis Varoufakis
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: India’s Unfinished Reform Agenda
content: Elevated oil prices, potentially strong El Niño conditions during the monsoon season, and the tariff-induced fragmentation of global trade are bearing down on the Indian economy and will likely be a drag on GDP growth. But these adverse conditions may be a blessing in disguise if they force India to complete its economic reform agenda.

creator: Shishir Gupta
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Middle East’s New Normal
content: The most likely outcome of the Iran war is not peace but an unsatisfying ceasefire that leaves the underlying issues unresolved. This will mean an uneasy equilibrium in which Gulf countries go their own way, US influence wanes, and the regional order remains unsettled.
snippet: The most likely outcome of the Iran war is not peace but an unsatisfying ceasefire that leaves the underlying issues unresolved. This will mean an uneasy equilibrium in which Gulf countries go their
creator: Don Aviv
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: No Bridge Over Healing Waters for the G7
content: The French G7 presidency’s focus on global imbalances is both welcome and problematic. While long-standing trade imbalances between the United States, Europe, and China are, if anything, becoming even more troublesome, the leaders’ summit in Évian-les-Bains will do nothing to address the problem.
snippet: The French G7 presidency’s focus on global imbalances is both welcome and problematic. While long-standing trade imbalances between the United States, Europe, and China are, if anything, becoming even
creator: Barry Eichengreen
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Are Government Stakes the Key to AI Sovereignty?
content: At first glance, reports that the US and Chinese governments are both considering taking stakes in national AI champions would seem to suggest a convergence between the world’s two leading AI powers. But a closer look reveals stark differences in their underlying strategies.
snippet: At first glance, reports that the US and Chinese governments are both considering taking stakes in national AI champions would seem to suggest a convergence between the world’s two leading AI powers.
creator: Angela Huyue Zhang
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What Next for Global Trade?
creator: Pinelopi Koujianou Goldberg
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Developing-Country Risk Is Being Mispriced
content: When investors say there are no “bankable” projects in developing economies, they are typically referring less to project quality than to the cost of capital. But the assumptions driving up risk premia are not supported by the evidence, and until this changes, the misallocation of capital will continue to undermine development.
snippet: When investors say there are no “bankable” projects in developing economies, they are typically referring less to project quality than to the cost of capital. But the assumptions driving up risk
creator: Vera Songwe
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: From the American Revolution to Universal Suffrage
content: Britain’s attempt to crush the American Revolution exposed a constitutional crisis at the heart of its empire. One English statesman saw in it proof that only broader suffrage could check royal power, tame corruption, and ensure that imperial rule remained compatible with liberty.
snippet: Britain’s attempt to crush the American Revolution exposed a constitutional crisis at the heart of its empire. One English statesman saw in it proof that only broader suffrage could check royal power,
creator: Danielle Allen
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Jefferson’s Revolutionary Language
content: Of the delegates who assembled in Philadelphia in 1776 for the Second Continental Congress, only Thomas Jefferson possessed the skill, knowledge, and linguistic gifts to make the Declaration of Independence an immortal text. He was the self-appointed conscience of America, and sensational imagery was his stock-in-trade.
snippet: Of the delegates who assembled in Philadelphia in 1776 for the Second Continental Congress, only Thomas Jefferson possessed the skill, knowledge, and linguistic gifts to make the Declaration of
creator: Andrew Burstein
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Declaration of the Enslaved
content: For African-Americans, the Declaration of Independence was nothing but a “thin veil to cover the crimes” of slavery, as Frederick Douglass put it. Inspired by Haiti’s Declaration of Independence, free and enslaved Black people in the US found themselves subverting America’s founding document to achieve their own emancipation.
snippet: For African-Americans, the Declaration of Independence was nothing but a “thin veil to cover the crimes” of slavery, as Frederick Douglass put it. Inspired by Haiti’s Declaration of Independence, free
creator: Sudhir Hazareesingh
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Free-Market Capitalism Is Destroying US Democracy
content: Far from two sides of the same coin, free-market capitalism and democracy are fundamentally incompatible. The only way to restore US democracy is to transform the system, so that it compensates innovators but limits their market power, while ensuring that the benefits of economic progress are more equitably shared.
snippet: Far from two sides of the same coin, free-market capitalism and democracy are fundamentally incompatible. The only way to restore US democracy is to transform the system, so that it compensates
creator: Mordecai Kurz
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Canada’s Great Recalibration
content: The growing fusion of economics, security, and technology has altered the nature of 21st-century interdependence, and Canada has responded by being more intentional. Rather than automatically serving as an extension of US-centered systems, it is creating more options across a wider network of relationships.
snippet: The growing fusion of economics, security, and technology has altered the nature of 21st-century interdependence, and Canada has responded by being more intentional. Rather than automatically serving
creator: Paul Samson
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: From Jefferson to Nehru
content: The Declaration of Independence continues to challenge countries, including the US and India, to measure themselves against the ideals they profess. But in a century marked by rising inequality, an authoritarian resurgence, and the emergence of new forms of domination, measuring up will become only more difficult.
snippet: The Declaration of Independence continues to challenge countries, including the US and India, to measure themselves against the ideals they profess. But in a century marked by rising inequality, an
creator: Shashi Tharoor
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Roots of Democratic Decline
content: Notwithstanding recent setbacks for illiberal, populist, and authoritarian leaders in some countries, good-governance groups and experts on democratic decline continue to document a disturbing global trend toward “autocratization.” What remains to be seen is whether enough voters will wake up to the threat.
snippet: Notwithstanding recent setbacks for illiberal, populist, and authoritarian leaders in some countries, good-governance groups and experts on democratic decline continue to document a disturbing global
creator: Zehra F. Kabasakal Arat
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Standard of Independence
content: Although revolutions would surely have occurred had the United States never broken from Britain in 1776, the Declaration of Independence gave later anticolonial movements inspiration and the language to shape their own aspirations. It offered a universal blueprint, even as the US itself adopted the ethos of empire.
snippet: Although revolutions would surely have occurred had the United States never broken from Britain in 1776, the Declaration of Independence gave later anticolonial movements inspiration and the language
creator: David Armitage
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Declaration of Independence Turns South
content: Whether they were agreeing or arguing, the new countries that emerged across the Americas after the American Revolution influenced each other. Together, they helped shape the global development of modern democracy and the social and political movements that have shaped it ever since.
snippet: Whether they were agreeing or arguing, the new countries that emerged across the Americas after the American Revolution influenced each other. Together, they helped shape the global development of
creator: Caitlin Fitz
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Capitalism’s Creature
content: The American Dream, with its basis in Thomas Jefferson’s immortal words, was one of the most powerfully generative ideas ever conceived. But the economy and impersonal systems it helped to bring into being are now shaping society and its individual members, rather than the other way around.
